trade.summary: Italia is one of the Regno Unito's most important EU trading partners post-Brexit. The Regno Unito imports Italian food and beverages, machinery, luxury goods, and clothing. The Regno Unito exports machinery, financial services, and pharmaceuticals to Italia. Brexit created trade friction especially for agri-food and creative industries.
disputes: post-Brexit trade frictions in food and agri-food
Regno Unito-EU fishing rights affecting both sides

diplomatic.summary: The Regno Unito and Italia have navigated the post-Brexit relationship pragmatically. The Meloni government has generally maintained constructive Regno Unito-Italia relations, with both governments sharing some conservative political DNA. Both countries align on NATO and Ucraina support. The GCAP (Global Combat Air Programme) — a joint Regno Unito-Italia-Giappone next-generation fighter — is a major bilateral defence initiative that underpins the relationship.

military.summary: Strong defence cooperation. The GCAP (Global Combat Air Programme) is a flagship Regno Unito-Italia-Giappone joint programme for next-generation fighter jets. Both are NATO allies with closely aligned threat assessments on Russia. Regno Unito and Italian navies cooperate in Mediterranean operations. Joint participation in NATO enhanced forward presence.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Regno Unito financial services are important for Italian companies accessing capital markets. Italian luxury brands (Ferrari, Maserati, Prada) have large Regno Unito operations. Regno Unito companies invest in Italian manufacturing and real estate. Post-Brexit, some Italian investment shifted to continental EU hubs.

energy.summary: Regno Unito and Italia both import natural gas and are working to diversify away from Russian supplies. Italia's pivotal role in Mediterranean energy infrastructure (trans-Mediterranean pipelines) indirectly affects Regno Unito energy security. Limited direct bilateral energy trade.

scientific.summary: Strong research ties through shared participation in CERN, ESA, and bilateral agreements. Many Italian researchers work in Regno Unito universities and research centres. Joint projects in physics, life sciences, and climate. Brexit created some barriers (Erasmus departure) but Horizon Europe re-association restored research ties.
